meanings.definition: the capacity to attract and hold something
strip sewn over or along an edge for reinforcement or decoration
the act of applying a bandage
one of a pair of mechanical devices that are attached to a ski and that will grip a ski boot; the bindings should release in case of a fall
the protective covering on the front, back, and spine of a book
examples: the book had a leather binding
Will this wallpaper adhere to the wall?
The grandparents want to bond with the child

meanings.definition: make fast; tie or secure, with or as if with a rope
examples: The Chinese would bind the feet of their women

meanings.definition: wrap around with something so as to cover or enclose
secure with or as if with ropes
examples: tie up the old newspapers and bring them to the recycling shed

meanings.definition: bind by an obligation; cause to be indebted
examples: I'll hold you by your promise

meanings.definition: fasten or secure with a rope, string, or cord
examples: They tied their victim to the chair
These foods tend to constipate you
